Wake with Clay Pigeon: Playlist from July 9, 2026

View Clay Pigeon's profile

WFMU's morning show, featuring new technology that will sonically force caffeine directly into your bloodstream. Hosted by Clay Pigeon.


Click here to subscribe to the Wake n' Bake Email Newsletter.


Click here to subscribe to the Wake and Bake podcast and have all of each week's Wake and Bake segments delivered to you every Monday.


The Wake and Bake Answering Machine (WABAM) is now at this number: 646-494-1095. Call and leave a message for the show!

Monday-Friday 6 - 9am (EDT) | On WFMU | 91.1, 90.1, 91.9 FM & wfmu.org

Jerry in NYC:

Bonnie Tyler, a Welsh singer known for her stirring power ballads, died aged 75. Having started singing as a teenager, Ms Tyler’s trademark gravelly tone came about when she permanently damaged her vocal cords by screaming while recovering from surgery. She went on to achieve stardom with hits like “Total Eclipse of the Heart”.
♥ 7:04am

The ones in NJ are in Avalon, a town in Cape May County where they left a few miles of undeveloped shoreline, with spring swamps & such -- so that would be the ecosystem to look for. I think it's the spring swamps that allow them to breed.

Big Hairy Guy:

↳ Terr DE @7:08
Bobwhite calls were a daily occurrence thirty years ago around here (Cape Cod) until the state discontinued the stocking program. Many years passed before I heard one again but in the past ten years or so I hear them occasionally. Last time was last Fall when I actually saw one calling while standing on a fence post. Such beauties!
♥ 7:17am

beedee:

↳ Song: "Sam" by "Meat Puppets"
Love love love. Saw them on Forbidden Places tour. Great time in their era. Songs from all over the catalog. Boom boom boom maybe 30-40 songs in the set. Tight quick arrangements. Lean and mean meat machine. Hope they come back around again. 10 years ago in Asbury Park at the Lanes was great!
♥ 8:52am

¡Daybreak!:

Hey all greeting from the Mississippi River!
After 22 miles yesterday we spent the night moored by 2 anchors and a tree line over to the Girvin Conservation Area which is on the Missouri side of the Missouri/Tenessee boarder.
If any of you have Instagram and want to follow our trip. My captain is Chip Phillips @NorthernSon2026

I, your go-for mate, am @curtiscompendium on Instagram.

Maybe we'll see you over there today!
♥ 8:56am
Bob Tankenstein:

Thank you CLAY AND PJ AND WAKE TEAM
  8:56am
K2:

↳ Clay Pigeon @8:54
☮️☯️☮️ tanks fer being
8:56am
beedee:

↳ Anne @8:51
My wife and I were walking around on vacation somewhere and in the crowd a young boy noticed the brightly colored, one of a kind Curt Kirkwood artwork of odd creatures on a Meat Puppets T-shirt I was wearing. And the words in BIG PRINT : "MEAT PUPPETS". He just pointed and kept shouting in a VERY happy voice "MEAT PUPPETS! MEAT PUPPETS!" Glad I made his day. It is quite a fun band name to say for a young child. Hey kid, we're all just meat puppets. Skin and bone... with fillings
♥ 8:57am
Chris in Kensington Bklyn:

Thanks, CP, Paul and Wake crew and fellow Glisteners!! Make those Thursdays Thoroughly Moderne.
♥ 8:57am
